output:
**[REASONING CONTEXT]**
Amplify: minimum assumption selection; parsimony scoring; description length computation; epicycle detection
Suppress: over-engineering bias; unnecessary hypothesis generation; variable proliferation; ad hoc explanation stacking
**[END REASONING CONTEXT]**

Applying the ability: rank each explanation by the number and specificity of technical constructs it requires. Fewer/vaguer constructs = more abstract.

**E3** — "old and heavily used": zero technical vocabulary, maximum vagueness. No domain knowledge assumed. **Most abstract.**

**E2** — "metal fatigue after 40 years of cyclic loading": engineering-level terminology (metal fatigue, cyclic loading), one quantitative data point. **Intermediate.**

**E1** — "iron atoms... BCC crystal lattice... grain boundaries... critical Griffith length": atomic/crystallographic resolution, materials science formalism, precise mechanisms. **Most concrete.**

The answer is **(A) E3, E2, E1**.
